key_white_space: No competitor combines non-technical founder audience + live AI builds + practitioner-led pedagogy + AI-native infrastructure (Zo credits, Zo-to-Zo, concierge, hotline) + Meta OS heuristic pedagogy + personal curation (V interviews every applicant for intellectual chops, AI passion, and collaborative capacity). Hampton/YPO/EO are peer communities with zero AI/building component. Reforge/Section teach frameworks but don't build live. Buildspace/Latent Space serve technical builders. FounderMaxxing occupies the intersection of all six dimensions simultaneously.
top_conversion_insights: Hero line: 'In your rush to keep your company ahead, you risk falling behind on AI.' — collapses the tension into one sentence. Largest type on the page.

Curation IS the product: V interviews every applicant for intellectual chops, genuine AI passion, and collaborative capacity. The vetting process signals room quality. Application-only, invite-only for now.

Three-part proof structure: (A) Split testimonial gallery — technical track and non-technical track with name/title/company placards, (B) Published articles as preview cards, (C) Link to /pedagogy page for the intellectually curious.

Zo: Attention Is All You Need — reference to the foundational Transformer paper. Handles the 'why Zo?' question head-on. Backed by four honest reasons: V's relationship with Zo founders, non-technical-first platform, $100 credits, transferable design patterns.

Pedagogy page (/pedagogy) as conversion escape valve — houses Meta OS philosophy, decomposition pattern, context engineering levels, offensive/defensive prompting, and building blocks. Visually driven. The landing page sells the room; the pedagogy page sells the thinking.
Price hidden or anchored: At $100/mo, FounderMaxxing is underpriced vs comp set — anchor against consultant cost ($5K-15K/mo) and Hampton (~$700/mo). Frame $100 Zo credits as 'first month costs $0.'
One offer at launch: Only Founding 15 visible. No tier comparison grid. Single clear ask.
